mapping.a_element: the prearranged correlated (entangled) quantum systems used as the teleportation resource
mapping.b_element: the bipartite entangled Gaussian states whose entanglement is being characterized

mapping.a_element: the continuous-variable system whose state is teleported
mapping.b_element: the oscillator (continuous-variable) modes on Alice's and Bob's sides

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the shared objects (bipartite entangled, continuous-variable/oscillator systems), not shared machinery: paper A is a dynamical transfer PROTOCOL that moves an unknown state via nonlocal measurement plus classical communication, whereas paper B is a static CLASSIFICATION of separability using the covariance-matrix positivity of the partial transpose (PPT criterion and bound entanglement). There is no role-for-role correspondence between a teleportation procedure and a partial-transpose separability test, so the underlying methods differ.
